raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.0,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The section makes a causal attribution ('we attribute these gains to the cross-attention refinement module') supported only by an ablation forward-reference, draws a sweeping conclusion ('strongest unified segmentation model'), and appends a loosely-coupled panoptic-track result as corroboration — all requiring logical coherence checks.","R1.3":"The artifact is an empirical results section built entirely on specific mIoU and PQ figures compared to named baselines; solidity here is meaningless unless claims are grounded in actual experiments, tables, and cited figures.","R1.4":"Four SOTA claims plus 'strongest unified segmentation model on standard benchmarks' constitute broad superiority assertions; one benchmark shows only a match ('matches the previous SOTA') and COCO-Stuff lacks an explicit prior-work comparison, making delta-vs-prior-work scrutiny central.","R1.7":"InternImage-H and ViT-Adapter are invoked by name as comparison anchors without reference markers; the COCO-Stuff panoptic baseline is unnamed entirely; solidity requires verifying these attributions and flagging coverage gaps."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Does 'solid' mean primarily (a) that the numerical benchmark figures and competitor attributions are factually correct and traceable to source papers, or (b) that the argumentative logic connecting results to conclusions is coherent and free of unsupported causal leaps?"}

rationale.R1.2: The section makes a causal attribution ('we attribute these gains to the cross-attention refinement module') supported only by an ablation forward-reference, draws a sweeping conclusion ('strongest unified segmentation model'), and appends a loosely-coupled panoptic-track result as corroboration — all requiring logical coherence checks.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact is an empirical results section built entirely on specific mIoU and PQ figures compared to named baselines; solidity here is meaningless unless claims are grounded in actual experiments, tables, and cited figures.
rationale.R1.4: Four SOTA claims plus 'strongest unified segmentation model on standard benchmarks' constitute broad superiority assertions; one benchmark shows only a match ('matches the previous SOTA') and COCO-Stuff lacks an explicit prior-work comparison, making delta-vs-prior-work scrutiny central.
rationale.R1.7: InternImage-H and ViT-Adapter are invoked by name as comparison anchors without reference markers; the COCO-Stuff panoptic baseline is unnamed entirely; solidity requires verifying these attributions and flagging coverage gaps.

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'solid' mean primarily (a) that the numerical benchmark figures and competitor attributions are factually correct and traceable to source papers, or (b) that the argumentative logic connecting results to conclusions is coherent and free of unsupported causal leaps?
